banner.title: Gemini Omni Pricing and Credits
banner.description: Understand how DreamOmni pricing works: free signup credits for trying the product, paid subscription tiers for recurring creation, and credit packs for teams that need extra AI video or image generation capacity.

tabs.title: Create Before You Scale
tabs.subTitle: Prompt
tabs.placeholder: Describe a short video you want to generate before choosing a larger credit plan...

tabs.title: Image to Video
tabs.subTitle: Image
tabs.placeholder: Upload an image to estimate whether this workflow fits your project
tabs.tips: Upload JPG, PNG, WEBP, or GIF images up to 20MB.

details.title: Start Free, Then Scale with Credits
details.description: DreamOmni uses a credit-based model so different generation workflows can have different costs. New users can start with free credits, then move to subscriptions or credit bundles when they need more output, higher volume, or repeated campaign work.

cells.content: Try a few prompts and compare text-to-video vs image-to-video
A predictable credit allowance each billing cycle
Extra credits for launches, content batches, and client work

details.title: Current Subscription Ladder in This Product
details.description: The active product configuration includes Mini, Standard, and Plus subscription tiers. The exact checkout price and availability can vary by payment provider and region, so use the live pricing modal as the final source before purchase.

cells.content: Occasional testing and small creative batches
Steady content production and creator workflows

details.title: Budgeting for AI Video Generation
details.description: AI video costs more than text or static image generation because each clip requires many frames, motion coherence, and often audio or reference processing. Budget by finished clips, not only by prompts, because revisions and failed creative directions are part of real production.

cells.content: Describe subject, motion, camera, style, duration, and negative constraints up front
Use images to reduce ambiguity when identity or product appearance matters
Set a cap per concept before generating many variants

usageGuide.title: How to Choose a DreamOmni Plan
usageGuide.description: Match plan size to how often you create, how many variants you need, and whether you are experimenting or producing finished assets.

steps.title: Use the free credits first
steps.description: Test the core workflows and note how many credits your preferred generation type uses.

steps.title: Estimate monthly output
steps.description: Count expected finished clips, draft variants, and prompt revisions rather than only final downloads.

steps.title: Pick subscription or pack
steps.description: Choose a recurring plan for steady work, or a one-time credit pack for a short production burst.

faq.name: faq
faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Gemini Omni Pricing Questions
faq.description: Answers about credits, plans, and budgeting.

items.title: Is DreamOmni free?
items.description: DreamOmni offers a free starting path with signup credits. Larger or repeated generation workloads require paid credits or a subscription.

items.title: What are credits?
items.description: Credits are the unit DreamOmni uses to pay for AI generation workflows. Different models and settings can require different credit amounts.

items.title: Which plan should I choose?
items.description: Start free, estimate your monthly generation volume, then choose a subscription for recurring work or a credit pack for a one-time campaign.

items.title: Is this the same as Google API pricing?
items.description: No. DreamOmni pricing is product credit pricing. Official Google Gemini API or Vertex AI video pricing is separate and may be billed per second.

cta.title: Start Free, Then Choose the Right Credit Path
cta.description: Try the generator first so your plan decision is grounded in real output.
